title: Coelogyne ambangensis
intro: Coelogyne ambangensis, also known as the Ambang Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to the island of Borneo. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne ambangensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coelogyne ambangensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to aid in the treatment of respiratory infections and other conditions.
taxonomy: Coelogyne ambangensis is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This species is further categorized within the subfamily Epidendroideae, a subfamily of orchids that includes around 30,000 species. Within the genus Coelogyne, Coelogyne ambangensis is one of several species that are native to the tropical regions of Asia. The exact classification of Coelogyne ambangensis may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Further taxonomic classification may include the order Asparagales, which is the order that includes the Orchidaceae family.
